blockingIssues.description: web/package.json uses next ^15.3.0 while the feature contract requires Next.js 16.
contracts/sources/types.move is missing the required OrderBook key resource in the reviewed commit.
register_market storage shape diverges from feature contract (expected ProtocolState pair_id->address mapping, implemented separate MarketRegistry with pair_id->Market).
register_market only creates OrderBook once; subsequent market registrations do not get isolated fresh orderbooks.
IOC path cancels immediately without attempting fills, violating IOC partial-fill-then-cancel-remainder semantics.
Market orders lock/unlock and emit events but do not match against opposing liquidity.
FOK success path can rest orders on book instead of filling atomically or aborting.
order_type is not validated at entry boundary, allowing unsupported values.
Trade settlement path does not integrate maker/taker fee deductions and fee vault collection when fees are non-zero.
Delegated subaccount authorization is not wired into place-order entry paths.
Delegated subaccount authorization is not wired into cancel-order entry path.
get_user_balances public view signature diverges from the feature contract API shape.

appliedUpdates.description: Added commands.deploy-testnet = 'bash scripts/deploy-testnet.sh' so the new deployment workflow is available as shared operational command.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit cross-module integration checkpoint: when adding new modules (fees/subaccounts), require verification that existing entry/settlement flows call into them before marking feature complete.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: contract-views-fees-edge-cases added modules but settlement had no fees integration and entry/cancel flows had no delegation hook.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify Move testing gotchas: timestamp::set_time_has_started_for_testing requires @0x1 signer; add a short recipe to avoid repeated trial-and-error.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: contract-order-placement review and handoff feedback flagged this repeatedly.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance for friend declarations and cross-module visibility constraints in Move to reduce compile-debug loops during modular feature work.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: contract-views-fees-edge-cases handoff feedback requested this after integration friction.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Resolve market-scope guidance conflict by explicitly stating whether this mission is single-market CASH/USDC only or multi-market, and align AGENTS.md with .factory/library/architecture.md.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews found AGENTS.md language implying multi-pair support while architecture.md states single market.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten tests-first procedure wording or enforcement to match observed worker behavior, since multiple completed features implemented code before red-phase tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Repeated observation across contract-order-placement, contract-matching-settlement, and testnet-testcash-deploy reviews.

rejectedObservations.observation: move-worker skill lacks a note about exposing #[test_only] init_module_for_test helpers
rejectedObservations.reason: covered by broader recommended skill update on Move testing/integration guidance

rejectedObservations.observation: move-worker skill lacks note about returning references borrowed from global storage
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous as universal guidance without a concrete reproducible pattern in current contract code; defer to targeted skill maintenance

rejectedObservations.observation: Feature contract sequencing ambiguity around contract-order-placement matching dependency
rejectedObservations.reason: belongs to mission planning artifact (features.json), not AGENTS/services/library; tracked via blocking review findings instead

rejectedObservations.observation: Duplicate tests-first process drift reports from multiple reviews
